articles.title: Live Coverage: Third flight of Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket to feature 1st reuse of booster
articles.summary: Blue Origin plans to launch its third New Glenn rocket from Cape Canaveral Sunday during a two-hour window that opens at 6:45 a.m. EDT (1045 UTC). It will carry AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 satellite to provide direct to cell phone services from space.

articles.title: Antitrust Authority Approves Creation of Rheinmetall-OHB Satellite Joint Venture
articles.summary:
The Federal Cartel Office (Bundeskartellamt), Germany’s independent competition authority, has approved a new joint venture between Rheinmetall and OHB. In a 16 April announcement, the Bundeskartellamt explained that the new subsidiary will be used to bid for a German armed forces public procurement contract. In September 2025, the German government announced that it had committed […]
The post Antitrust Authority Approves Creation of Rheinmetall-OHB Satellite Joint Venture appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX attempts 600th Falcon booster landing amid West Coast Starlink mission
articles.summary: The Starlink 17-22 will see SpaceX launch 25 broadband internet satellites into low Earth orbit. Liftoff from pad 4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base is scheduled for 7:33:40 a.m. PDT (10:33:40 a.m. EDT / 1433:40 UTC).

articles.title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
articles.summary: NASA’s Human Research Program (HRP) uses research to develop methods to protect the health and performance of astronauts in space. In support of NASA’s goals for long-term missions on the surface of the Moon and human exploration of Mars, HRP is using ground research facilities, the International Space Station, and analog environments to monitor human health in deep space.  NASA’s Artemis II mission was the first […]
